content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game on a sequence of NNN positive integers, say A1,A2,...ANA_1, A_2, ... A_NA1​,A2​,...AN​ The game is played as follows.


If all the numbers are equal, the game ends.
Otherwise

Select two numbers which are unequal
Subtract the smaller number from the larger number
Replace the larger number with the result from above


Chef has already figured out that the game **always terminates**. He also knows, for a given sequence of integers, the game will always terminate on the **same value**, no matter how the game is played. Chef wants you to simulate the game for him and tell him if the game terminates on 111.

In fact, there may be many such games. Given a sequence of integers Chef wants to know the number of **sub-sequences** of the given sequence, for which, playing the above game on the subsuquence will terminate on 111. A **sub-sequence** can be obtained from the original sequence by **deleting 000 or more** integers from the original sequence. See the explanation section for clarity.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then follow the description of TTT test cases.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the sequence.
The second line contains NNN positive integers, each separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, **output a single integer** - the number of **sub-sequences** of the original sequence, such that, playing the game on the **sub-sequence** results in ending the game with all the values equal to 111.
